_comment2: ACS Deploy-time policy: require Cosign signature for production images
_comment3: OPTIONAL — requires Cosign signing key configured in pipeline (T3)
_comment4: Only enforced in production namespace; informational in lower envs

policies.name: DevSecOps - Require Image Signature (Deploy)
policies.description: Requires container images deployed to production to be signed with Cosign. This verifies image provenance and ensures only images that passed the full T3 pipeline are promoted.
policies.rationale: Image signing provides cryptographic proof that the image was built by a trusted CI pipeline and has not been tampered with. This is a key supply chain security control.
policies.remediation: Run the T3 (tag) pipeline to build and sign the image with Cosign. The signImage.groovy function in the shared library handles signing. Ensure the cosign-signing-key credential is configured in Jenkins.
policies._note: Disabled by default — enable after Cosign signing key is configured and signature integration is set up in ACS. Set disabled=false and configure a Cosign signature integration in ACS to activate.
